page: Google Cloud Platform e2-medium is an entry-level, shared-core virtual machine instance configured with 2 vCPUs mapped to a single physical core with 2 threads per core and 4.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor operating at 2.2 GHz, the instance features a CPU cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 16 MB L3 cache. The CPU allocation is shared, meaning physical core resources are multiplexed. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations reveal a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% due to the shared-core architecture. Memory bandwidth is highly constrained, with read-only large block speeds at 6.36 GB/sec and write speeds at 4.83 GB/sec, both ranking in the bottom 10%. Similarly, database operations, Redis SET performance at 0.82 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 0.63 GB/sec are limited, reflecting its resource-constrained profile.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the e2-medium offers a highly economical option for workloads that do not require sustained, high-performance compute or memory bandwidth. The shared-core model provides a low-cost entry point for development environments, lightweight microservices, basic web serving, and low-traffic applications. It is not suitable for memory-intensive databases, high-throughput data processing, or complex multi-threaded applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-medium is a budget-friendly, shared-core virtual machine designed for lightweight workloads. It features an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.2 GHz with 2 vCPUs sharing a single physical core, alongside 4.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show a strong single-core CPU capability, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, but multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are highly constrained, falling into the bottom 10% to 25% across most metrics. This resource profile results in low database and web serving throughput, with Redis SET operations limited to 0.82 million operations per second. Consequently, the e2-medium is best suited for low-traffic web servers, development environments, and small microservices where cost efficiency is prioritized over sustained high performance.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-medium is a shared-core virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor, designed as a cost-efficient option for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-medium is a shared-core virtual machine with 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB RAM, ideal for low-traffic web serving and development.
tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-core virtual instance offering basic compute and memory resources for lightweight applications and development environments.
bullet_points: Shared-core architecture with 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB RAM
Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.2 GHz
Strong single-core CPU performance but limited multi-core scaling
Highly constrained memory bandwidth and database throughput
No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware included
Best suited for development environments and low-traffic microservices
